I

Getting to Know HubSpot

This section covers the basics of HubSpot. If you've never used a CRM before, read this section carefully before moving on. If you already know HubSpot, skip ahead to Section II.

What is a CRM?

A CRM (Customer Relationship Management) is a system where you keep track of every person you work with, every conversation you've had, and every step of the process. Think of it as a shared notebook that the entire team can see - except it's organized, searchable, and it remembers everything automatically.

At Finnita, HubSpot is the CRM. Every contact, every deal, every phone call, every payment - it all lives here. When you log a call, your coworkers can see it. When you create a subscription, the system sends the invoice. Nothing happens outside of HubSpot.

Navigating the Interface

1The Left Sidebar

The left sidebar is your main menu. Click the CRM icon (the second icon from the top) to see all the sections you'll use daily:

Here's what each section is for:

SectionWhat it isWhen you use it
ContactsPeople - every lead, prospect, and clientTo look up a person, check their info, or update their record
CompaniesEmployers - the organizations that contract FinnitaTo see which employer a contact belongs to
DealsEnrollment processes - one deal per clientTo track where each client is in the enrollment process
TasksYour to-do list - calls to make, follow-ups to doEvery day, to see who you need to call
CallsPhone call logTo review past calls or see call history
PlaybooksCall scripts and guided workflowsDuring calls, to follow the right steps

What is a Contact?

2The Contact Record

A contact is a person in HubSpot. Every employee who registers through the Finnita app or landing page becomes a contact. When you open a contact, you see three main areas:

  • Left panel - "About this contact": name, email, phone, address, and all personal properties
  • Center area - Overview tab with organized sections of data (loan info, client information, benefit details), and Activities tab with the full history
  • Right panel - Associations: linked companies, deals, payments, invoices, and subscriptions

Notice the action buttons at the top of the left panel - Note, Email, Call, Task, Meeting, More. These are how you interact with the contact. You'll use "Call" and "Task" the most.

3Searching for a Contact

There are two ways to find a contact:

  • Search bar (top of the page) - Type a name, email, or phone number. HubSpot searches across all records.
  • Contacts list - Go to CRM > Contacts. You'll see all contacts in a table. Use the filters at the top to narrow down by owner, date, or any property.

What are Properties?

4Properties are Fields

Every piece of information in HubSpot is stored in a property (also called a field). For example, a contact has properties like "First Name", "Email", "Loan Status", "Total Loan Balance".

To edit a property, click on it in the contact or deal record. Type the new value and click away or press Enter to save. Some properties are dropdowns (you pick from a list), some are text fields, some are dates.

At Finnita, many properties are filled automatically when a lead comes in through the app. But when data is missing - because the person dropped off the app before finishing - you'll need to fill in the blanks during your call.

What is a Deal?

5Deals Track the Enrollment Process

A deal represents one client's enrollment journey with Finnita. There's one deal per client. The deal moves through stages - like checkpoints on a road. Each stage means something specific happened:

  • New Deal - The person just registered, nothing has happened yet
  • Appointment Scheduled - A call has been booked
  • Assessment Completed - All financial data collected, plans explained
  • Paid - First payment received
  • Ready to Enroll - Everything confirmed, ready for the broker
  • Submitted to Processing - Sent to the broker
  • Program Active - Done. Client is enrolled and paying monthly.

You can see all deals in the board view - each column is a stage, and each card is a deal. You move deals by dragging them from one column to another:

6The Deal Record

When you open a deal, the layout is similar to a contact - left panel with deal properties, center with timeline, right panel with associations. The deal record will display the same properties as the contact record, and they are automatically copied from the associated contact.

The right panel shows everything connected to this deal - the contact, the employer company, any invoices, payments, and subscriptions:

This is how you check if a client has paid, what subscription they have, and which company they belong to - all without leaving the deal page.

What is a Company?

7Companies are Employers

In Finnita's HubSpot, a company is an employer - like "Athens ISD" or "Norstan Communication". Companies are linked to contacts (the employees) and to deals. Each company has a Client ID that identifies them uniquely.

You will not have access to create or edit companies - the Refindable team manages that for you. But you'll see them in the right panel of contacts and deals, and you'll need the company name when searching for the right products to add to a subscription. If you need a company created or updated, reach out to the Refindable team.

What are Tasks?

8Tasks are Your To-Do List

A task is something you need to do - usually "call this person". Tasks are created automatically when a new deal is created. If you need to create a task manually, always do it from within a deal record so it is automatically associated with that deal.

Your Tasks view is filtered by "Assigned to: Me" so you only see your own work. Each task shows the title, the associated contact, and the due date:

To create a task, open the deal record, click the "Task" button, set a title, due date, and assign it to yourself or someone else. Creating tasks from the deal ensures the task is automatically linked to the deal and its associated contact.

To complete a task, click the checkbox next to it - or open it and mark it as done.

What are Subscriptions and Invoices?

9Subscriptions Generate Invoices

A subscription is a billing setup. When you create a subscription, you're telling HubSpot: "charge this person X amount every month for Y months." HubSpot then automatically creates invoices - one for each billing period - and sends them to the client's email.

An invoice is a bill with a payment link. The client receives it by email, clicks the link, and pays with their credit card or bank account. After the first payment, their card is saved and future charges happen automatically.

You can see subscriptions and invoices in the right panel of any deal or contact:

Creating subscriptions is the most important part of your process - Section IV covers this step by step with screenshots.

The Timeline

10Everything Gets Logged

Every contact and every deal has a timeline (the Activities tab). This is a chronological record of everything that's happened - calls, emails, deal stage changes, workflow events, subscription activity. It's like a diary that writes itself.

Before calling someone, always check their timeline. It tells you:

  • Has anyone called them before? What happened?
  • Did they receive any emails?
  • When was their deal created? What stage changes happened?
  • Is there any subscription or payment activity?

This avoids calling someone and asking them the same questions they already answered, or missing important context about their situation.

II

Your Day-to-Day

YOUR DAY AT A GLANCE

Log in, check your task queue, each task is linked to a deal, open the deal to see what stage it's in, call the contact, complete missing data if needed, help them choose a plan, create the subscription, confirm next steps, move the deal to the next stage.

1Log In and Open Your Tasks

Go to app.hubspot.com and log in with your credentials. Then navigate to Tasks using the left sidebar under CRM.

In the Tasks view, filter by "Assigned to: Me". You'll see tasks like "New form submission – Contact the lead". These are your calls for the day. Start with the oldest ones first.

2Understand Your Tasks

A deal is always created when someone signs up through the app, no matter how far they got. A task is also always created when the deal is created - but the type of task depends on where the person dropped off:

ScenarioDeal StageYour Task
Completed full enrollment in appAssessment Completed or furtherReview call - verify info, confirm plan choice, create subscription
Dropped off during enrollmentNew Deal or Appointment ScheduledRe-engagement call - collect missing data, explain plans, guide through enrollment

The deal stage tells you what happened. The task tells you what to do next.

3Open the Deal and Review

Click on the task to open the associated deal. Before calling, check:

  • How they arrived - They always come through the Finnita App
  • What data is already filled - Loan info, employer, income
  • Previous activity - Check the timeline for past calls or emails
  • Deal stage - What point did they reach?

On the right panel, you can see associated contacts, companies, invoices, subscriptions, and payments.

4Open the Contact Record

Click the contact name in the deal's right panel to see their full record. This shows all their personal and financial information:

Switch to the Activities tab to see the full history - calls, emails, workflow events, deal changes:

III

Making Calls

1Start the Call

From the contact record, click the Call button in the action bar:

The HubSpot dialer opens. Your number is already selected. Click the green phone button to dial:

2During the Call

Once connected, you'll see the call panel. You can record, mute, take notes, and use the assistant - all while the call is active:

If you want to record the call, click Record. HubSpot will show a consent warning first - you must inform the other party before recording:

3After the Call: Log It

When the call ends, select the Outcome - this is how HubSpot tracks what happened:

Add your notes about the call - what was discussed, what the client needs, any follow-up actions:

The call gets logged automatically in the contact's timeline with outcome, duration, and your notes:

IV

Creating Subscriptions

CRITICAL SECTION - READ CAREFULLY

Creating a subscription is the most important action you'll perform. It triggers real charges to the client. Double-check every field before saving. An error here means the client gets charged incorrectly.

When a client is ready to enroll, you create 1 subscription in HubSpot that includes 2 products: the Certification Fee (one-time) and the Monthly Fee (recurring). HubSpot then automatically generates invoices and sends them to the client by email.

1Open the Create Subscription Screen

From the contact or deal record, find Subscriptions in the right panel and click "Add". The Create Subscription screen opens. First, add the billing contact by clicking "+Add contact":

Once the contact is added, you'll see their name, email, and phone:

2Add the Products

Scroll down to Line Items and click "Select from product library":

The product library opens. Search for the employer's name (e.g., type "sabin" for Sabine ISD). Select both products - the Certification Fee AND the Monthly Fee. Then click Add:

COMMON MISTAKES TO AVOID
  • Wrong employer product - Each employer has its own products. "Athens ISD - Monthly Fee" is NOT the same as "Harmony ISD - Monthly Fee". Search carefully.
  • Wrong amount - Most monthly fees are $32.95, but some employers have custom pricing ($29.95, $33.95). Always verify.
  • Wrong contact email - The invoice goes to the email on the subscription. Make sure it's correct.
3Set the Details

After adding the products, you'll see them listed with their pricing. Check:

  • Certification Fee: Billing Frequency = One-time, Number of payments = 1
  • Monthly Fee: Billing Frequency = Monthly, set the Billing start date

For Billing Terms on the Monthly Fee, always choose "Fixed number of payments":

Enter the number of months for the plan:

4Review the Summary

Scroll down to the Summary section. This shows you exactly what will be charged:

Key things to verify:

  • Due now - This is the first invoice: Cert Fee + first Monthly Fee (e.g., $407 + $32.95 = $439.95)
  • Future payments - $32.95/month starting the following month
  • Total - The full contract value
  • Payment collection - Should be "Collect payment method on the first paid invoice" (the client pays the first invoice, their card is saved for future auto-charges)
5Checkout & Invoice Settings

Below the summary, verify the Checkout settings - online payments should be ON, accepting credit/debit cards and ACH:

Expand Invoice Settings at the bottom. Make sure Language is English, Locale is United States, and fill in the Client ID:

Once everything is verified, click "Create subscription" in the top-right corner. The subscription goes live immediately - the status is set to Active right away, even before the client pays. After one day without payment, the status changes to Past Due.

6After Creating the Subscription

Tell the client on the phone: "You'll receive an email with your invoice shortly. You'll have a few days to complete the payment. Please check your inbox and spam folder."

The subscription is automatically associated with the deal, the contact, and the company. You can verify it in the right panel:

Move the deal to the "Paid" stage once the first payment is received, then continue moving through stages until Program Active.

V

When Things Go Wrong

Client Doesn't Pay

1Failed Payment Flow

If a monthly payment fails, here's what happens automatically:

TimelineSubscription StatusDealAction
Payment failsPast DueStays in Program ActiveNotification sent to Call Center
Grace periodPast Due (still Active on deal)Stays in Program ActiveNotification sent to Call Center
After grace periodCancelledStays in Program Active, but subscription shows InactiveNotification sent to Call Center

The deal does NOT move out of Program Active (Closed Won). What changes is the subscription status - it goes from Active to Past Due to Cancelled. This is all handled automatically by HubSpot. You don't need to do anything manually.

VI

Reference & Glossary

Sales Pipeline Stages

Sales Pipeline
New Deal→ Appointment Scheduled→ Assessment Completed→ Paid→ Ready to Enroll→ Submitted to Processing→ Program Active
Suspended Client Closed Lost

You can drag deals between stages in the board view:

Glossary

TermWhat it means
Broker / ServicerCompany like Gotzoom that provides repayment plans. Finnita connects clients to brokers.
PSLFPublic Service Loan Forgiveness - forgives remaining balance after 120 qualifying payments for public service workers.
Certification FeeOne-time fee (~$407) when a client enrolls.
Monthly FeeRecurring monthly charge (~$32.95) for Finnita's service.
501(c)(3)Nonprofit status. Employees of 501(c)(3) organizations may qualify for PSLF.
ForbearanceTemporary pause on loan payments.
DefermentTemporary postponement of loan payments with specific eligibility criteria.
GarnishmentGovernment takes money directly from paychecks for defaulted loans.
ConsolidationCombining multiple federal loans into one.
GotzoomPrimary broker/servicer for Finnita clients.
ForthFinnita's previous CRM. Historical data was migrated to HubSpot.

ItemValue
Standard Certification Fee$407.00 (varies by employer)
Standard Monthly Fee$32.95/month (varies: $29.95 - $33.95)
Max call attempts per lead5
Total contacts in CRM~7,900
Total employers122
